title: Service entrypoint contract

p3_observations.title: Drift unit test omits negative cases for several no-write/boundary flags
p3_observations.line_or_area: Permission And Audit Binding validator drift test
p3_observations.issue: Claude noted the validator rejects drift test omitted unit-level negative cases for doesNotMutateEntityRegistry, doesNotReplaceHumanApproval, doesNotWriteDatabase, doesNotWriteStorage, doesNotPersistProductState, and array drift for evidence refs, context ids, schema fields, and acceptance gates.
p3_observations.recommendation: Extend the override table with no-write and array-drift cases.
p3_observations.resolution: Added negative drift cases for entity registry mutation, human approval replacement, database write, storage write, product-state persistence, required evidence drift, binding context drift, schema expectation drift, and acceptance gate drift.

review_text: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS. The S01 Permission And Audit Binding service entrypoint contract is a correctly scoped, metadata-only addition that satisfies every required criterion and is internally consistent across contract, policy, service module, validator, fixture, README, RP00 validator, and tests. The single P3 finding concerned partial unit-level negative-test coverage and was fixed before production_ready.
deferred_or_informational: Claude verified dependency on RP00.P02.M04.S14 and production_ready closeout evidence refs.
Claude verified all eight binding contexts are declared and mirrored in acceptance gates.

Claude verified non-execution boundaries for runtime permission, AuthZ, permission engine, security trimming, audit ledger, audit event, database, storage, product-state persistence, and human approval replacement.
Claude verified RP00.P02.M05 remains open and hands off to RP00.P02.M05.S02 without overclaiming RP00.P02 or RP00 completion.

findings.title: Drift unit test omits negative cases for several no-write/boundary flags
findings.line_or_area: Permission And Audit Binding validator drift test
findings.details: The unit-level validator rejects drift test omitted several no-write and array-drift negative cases. RP00 validator already enforced these boundaries, so the finding was non-blocking.
